name: Chocoholic Cookies
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dark brown sugar; pack firm
preferably Lindt Swiss White Confectionery Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Butter 2 cookie sheets.
3. Do not alter the order in which the ingredients are combined.
4. In a large bowl, combine the oats and chocolate chips; set aside.
5. In another large bowl, beat together the butter and sugars until creamy.
6. Sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, and cocoa, then add to the butter mixture, stirring until thoroughly combined. The batter will be very stiff.
7. Stir the milk and vanilla into the eggs, then stir this mixture into the butter mixture until thoroughly combined.
8. Add the chips and oats; stir until mixed well.
9. Using a 2-tablespoon scoop, drop batter 2 inches apart on cookie sheets.
10. Bake for 9-12 minutes, or until cooked through.
11. Cool on pans for 1 minute; transfer to wire racks to cool completely.
12. Melt the white chocolate with the Crisco in the top of a double boiler over simmering water.
13. Holding a cooled cookie between your thumb and forefinger, dip the edge into the warm white chocolate to cover the top third of the cookie.
14. Place on a rack over wax paper to dry completely.
15. Store between layers of wax paper in an airtight container in a cool place.
